proposed_hypotheses.text: Each binary clinical feature has a non-zero main effect on pfs_months (i.e., mean pfs_months differs between feature=1 and feature=0).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months between feature=0 and feature=1 for each binary feature.
analyses.result_summary: feature_014 strongly negative (mean pfs 5.05 vs 3.70, diff=-1.35, p<1e-300); feature_027 strongly positive (4.12 vs 5.09, diff=+0.97, p~1.7e-216); feature_023 negative (-0.33, p~2e-58); feature_006 negative (-0.31, p~1e-49); feature_022 negative (-0.23, p~3e-7); feature_011 positive (+0.22, p=7.3e-4). feature_031, 010, 028, 016, 002, 012, 018, 020, 024 not significant.

analyses.code: ANOVA of pfs_months across feature_001 levels (0/1/2).
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotonic gradient: feature_001=0 mean=5.27, =1 mean=4.05, =2 mean=2.91. F=3528.9, p~0. feature_001 is a strong prognostic factor (likely an ordinal performance/severity score).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each continuous feature is correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Pearson and Spearman correlations of each continuous feature with pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 has very strong positive correlation (Pearson r=0.766, p~0); feature_025 negative (r=-0.123, p~2e-167); feature_019 positive (r=+0.102, p~3e-115); feature_005 weak negative (r=-0.038, p~3e-17). Other continuous features have only marginal correlations with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30–90) shows a monotonic positive dose–response on pfs_months across quartiles.

analyses.code: Quartile means of pfs_months by feature_015; OLS pfs_months ~ feature_015.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all features in a single multivariable OLS, the strongest independent predictors of pfs_months are feature_015 (positive), feature_001 (negative), feature_014 (negative), feature_019 (positive), feature_025 (negative), feature_023 (negative), feature_006 (negative), feature_027 (positive), and feature_022 (negative).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all 33 features.
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.859. Key coefficients (all p<1e-15 unless noted): feature_001 -1.17, feature_014 -1.36, feature_023 -0.35, feature_025 -0.076, feature_019 +0.469, feature_015 +0.176, feature_027 +0.943, feature_006 -0.287, feature_005 -0.005, feature_022 -0.356, feature_021 -0.0004. Most other features non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary features have no systematic confounding with the strongest prognostics (feature_001, feature_014, feature_015, feature_019), implying the dataset has random-treatment-like assignment for the binary indicators.

analyses.code: For each binary feature, mean values of feature_001, feature_014, feature_015, feature_019 in feature=1 vs feature=0.
analyses.result_summary: All binary features show near-balanced distribution of prognostic variables (e.g., for feature_027 feature_001 mean=0.79 in treated vs 0.80 overall; feature_015 mean=65.1 vs 65.0; feature_019 mean=3.80 vs 3.80). Treatment-like binaries are unconfounded by measured prognostics.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 has heterogeneous effects on pfs_months depending on feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, feature_005, and feature_011 (i.e., these features are predictive biomarkers for feature_027 effect).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + f + feature_027:f + key prognostics, for each f.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interactions of feature_027: with feature_023 (coef=-1.63, p~0); feature_006 (coef=-1.46, p~0); feature_022 (coef=-0.99, p~1e-103); feature_011 (coef=+0.89, p~2e-57); feature_005 (coef=-0.0258 per unit, p~0). These features act as effect modifiers of feature_027.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 modifies the effect of feature_027 positively: in patients with feature_011=1, feature_027 produces a larger increase in pfs_months than in patients with feature_011=0.

analyses.code: Stratified Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_027 within feature_011 strata.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=0: TE = +0.94 months (p~2e-199); feature_011=1: TE = +1.93 months (n_treated=313, p~3e-21). Doubling of effect when feature_011=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 is a predictive biomarker for feature_027: in patients with feature_023=1 the feature_027 treatment effect on pfs_months is abolished, while in feature_023=0 it remains large and positive.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by feature_027 stratified by feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: mean PFS 4.12 (control) vs 5.77 (treated), TE=+1.66 months (p~6e-293). feature_023=1: 4.12 vs 4.14, TE=+0.03 (p=0.46, n.s.). Effect entirely concentrated in feature_023=0 stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 is a predictive biomarker for feature_027: feature_027 effect on pfs_months is abolished in feature_006=1 patients.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by feature_027 stratified by feature_006.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is a predictive biomarker for feature_027: in feature_022=1 patients, feature_027 has no positive effect (or even a slightly negative effect) on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by feature_027 stratified by feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=0: 4.12 vs 5.14, TE=+1.03 (p~5e-226). feature_022=1: 4.13 vs 3.97, TE=-0.16 (p=0.13, n.s.). feature_022 abolishes feature_027 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 is a continuous predictive biomarker for feature_027: higher feature_005 progressively reduces the feature_027 treatment benefit on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by feature_027 within feature_005 tertiles (overall and within triple-negative subgroup).
analyses.result_summary: Overall: T1 (f005<2.66) TE=+1.78 (p~9e-185); T2 (2.66–7.39) TE=+1.10 (p~5e-93); T3 (>=7.40) TE=+0.007 (p=0.87). Within triple-negative cohort the gradient is even steeper: T1 TE=+5.05; T2 TE=+2.98; T3 TE≈0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative subgroup (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0), feature_027 produces an even larger PFS gain than in the full cohort.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_027 within feature_023=0 & feature_006=0 & feature_022=0 (n=17,372).
analyses.result_summary: Triple-negative cohort: pfs(off)=4.12, pfs(on)=6.88, TE=+2.76 months (p~0). Effect size more than doubles vs the marginal +0.97.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, and feature_011 are PURELY PREDICTIVE biomarkers (not prognostic): each has near-zero effect on pfs_months in untreated patients (feature_027=0) but strong effects in treated patients (feature_027=1).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests stratified by feature_027 (untreated vs treated cohorts) for each biomarker.
analyses.result_summary: In untreated (n=39,978): feature_023 diff=-0.001 (p=0.95); feature_006 -0.012 (p=0.59); feature_022 +0.010 (p=0.85); feature_011 +0.009 (p=0.89). In treated (n=10,022): feature_023 diff=-1.63 (p~1e-204); feature_006 -1.47 (p~3e-169); feature_022 -1.17 (p~1e-30); feature_011 +1.00 (p~1e-7). All four are pure predictive biomarkers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 is a prognostic factor that does not modify the relative effect of feature_027 (no interaction).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_001 + feature_027:feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 has strong main effect (-1.17 per level) in both untreated (5.08, 3.86, 2.73 across levels 0/1/2) and treated (6.01, 4.85, 3.63) cohorts; interaction coef=+0.005 (p=0.89). Pure prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 is a prognostic factor (large negative main effect) and acts approximately additively with feature_027 (no qualitative interaction).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by feature_014 within treated/untreated; multivariable OLS interaction.
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=0 vs 1: 4.87 vs 3.50 in untreated; 5.81 vs 4.51 in treated. Difference of differences ~+0.07 (n.s. in joint model). Treatment effect approximately preserved across feature_014 strata. Pure prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients meeting feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0, low feature_005 (e.g., < 5) defines a subgroup with markedly larger feature_027 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by feature_027 in triple-negative cohort, stratified by feature_005 cutoffs (1,2,3,5,7,10,15,20).
analyses.result_summary: Below-cutoff TEs: cut=1 +4.98; cut=2 +5.04; cut=3 +5.03; cut=5 +5.01; cut=7 +4.13. Above-cutoff TEs: cut=5 +0.005; cut=7 +0.02; cut=10 -0.02. Treatment benefit is essentially binary at feature_005≈5: patients below have ~+5 months; patients at or above gain nothing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative subgroup, feature_005 acts as a continuous effect modifier — feature_027 × feature_005 interaction is negative.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_005 + feature_027*feature_005 within triple-negative cohort.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 main coef = +3.35 (p~0), feature_005 main = +0.0002 (n.s.), interaction = -0.0649 per unit feature_005 (t=-25.0, p~0). Each unit of feature_005 erodes ~0.065 months of treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all candidate treatments, feature_027 is the unique feature with strong effect-modification signal; other binary features (feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_028, feature_002, feature_016, feature_031) show no meaningful heterogeneity.

analyses.code: For each binary feature, screen interactions with all other features after adjustment for f001, f014, f015. Record top |t-stat|.
analyses.result_summary: Top |t-stat| of best modifier per feature: feature_027 vs f023 |t|=79.5 (p~0); feature_023 vs f027 |t|=79.5; feature_006 vs f027 |t|=66.4; feature_022 vs f027 |t|=19.0; feature_011 vs f027 |t|=14.6; feature_014 vs f015 |t|=7.0; all others |t| ≤ 3.1 (p≥0.01). No second active treatment detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (f023=0, f006=0, f022=0, f005<5), no additional binary or continuous feature meaningfully further modifies feature_027 effect.

analyses.code: Within favorable cohort (n=9,326), screen feature_027 × f interactions for all remaining features.
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p-value within this cohort is feature_029 (p=0.073), then feature_015 (p=0.110); none reach Bonferroni-significance. The favorable subgroup is internally homogeneous.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complement of the favorable subgroup (any of feature_023=1, feature_006=1, feature_022=1, feature_005>=5) defines a population in which feature_027 produces no detectable PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_027 within complement of (f023=0 & f006=0 & f022=0 & f005<5).
analyses.result_summary: Complement (n=40,674; 8,119 treated): pfs(off)=4.12, pfs(on)=4.15, TE=+0.025 (p=0.33, n.s.). The favorable definition cleanly partitions responders from non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, and feature_005>=5 is independently necessary for the favorable subgroup definition: relaxing any single predicate substantially reduces the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Compute feature_027 TE in cohorts that drop one predicate at a time from (f023=0 & f006=0 & f022=0 & f005<5).
analyses.result_summary: All four predicates: TE=+5.01. Drop f023: +2.85. Drop f006: +3.30. Drop f022: +4.63. Drop f005<5: +2.76. Each predicate adds independent information; the largest individual contributions are from f023, f006, and f005.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1, layered on top of the favorable subgroup, does not add further predictive value beyond feature_023/006/022/005 (its earlier interaction signal is captured by the other markers).

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_027 within (f023=0 & f006=0 & f022=0 & f005<5), stratified by feature_011.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=0: TE=+5.00 (n=8,831). feature_011=1: TE=+5.24 (n=495). Difference small (+0.24) and not significant; feature_011 contributes minimal additional gain once the favorable subgroup is met.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A joint multivariable model combining prognostics and feature_027 × biomarker interactions captures the full pfs_months signal: prognostic main effects (feature_001, feature_014, feature_015, feature_019, feature_025) plus feature_027 main effect plus negative interactions with feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, feature_005.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_001 + feature_014 + feature_015 + feature_019 + feature_025 + feature_005 + feature_023 + feature_006 + feature_022 + feature_011 + feature_027 + (feature_027:f023) + (feature_027:f006) + (feature_027:f022) + (feature_027:f011) + (feature_027:f005).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The clinical interpretation is consistent with anti-EGFR therapy in metastatic CRC: feature_027 is anti-EGFR antibody therapy; the resistance markers feature_023, feature_006, feature_022 plausibly correspond to RAS mutation, right-sided primary, and BRAF V600E; feature_005 is a tumor-burden biomarker (CEA-like) where high values dampen benefit.

analyses.code: Pattern-matching summary across all preceding analyses.
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect is robustly +5 months in (f023=0 AND f006=0 AND f022=0 AND f005<5) and zero everywhere else; mutually exclusive resistance profile and a continuous tumor-burden modifier match the known biology of anti-EGFR antibodies in mCRC (RAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided, low CEA).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: In patients with feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5, feature_027 increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months (from ~4.1 to ~9.1); in the complement, feature_027 has no detectable effect.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_027 within favorable subgroup (n=9,326; 1,903 treated) and within its complement (n=40,674; 8,119 treated).
analyses.result_summary: Favorable: pfs(off)=4.088 ± 2.088, pfs(on)=9.101 ± 2.176, TE=+5.013 months, p~0 (n=9,326). Complement: TE=+0.025, p=0.33 (n=40,674). Net: feature_027 benefit is entirely concentrated in the predicted subgroup.
